title: SNAP PROGRAM - 211info
text: LAKEVIEW CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY SNAP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides monthly food benefits to help people buy food. Funds are issued through an Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) card, also called the Oregon Trail Card. 108 E Street N Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Eligibility is determined on a case-by-case basis. Individuals and families who: -Live in Oregon -Household income must be at or below 200% of the federal poverty level, based on monthly income before taxes -Are US citizens or eligible non-citizens Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 947-3376 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 947-2273 Main phone Child Welfare Office (541) 947-3376 Main phone Self-Sufficiency Office Main phone (541) 947-3376 Alternate (800) 699-9075 ONE Customer Service Center INTAKE PROCEDURE: Apply and complete an interview with an ODHS eligibility worker. Applications can be submitted online, by phone, in person, or using a paper application. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: No documents are required to submit an application, however, people may be asked to provide the following: -Identification -Proof of income -Social Security numbers for everyone in your household who wants benefits -Proof of immigration status for eligible non-citizens LANGUAGES: Phone: English, interpretation by arrangement. Applications: Available in English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, Somali AREA SERVED: Klamath and Lake Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Food Stamps/SNAP Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD ABUSE REPORTING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD WELFARE SERVICES MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER, ADOPT, AND KINSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SUMMER EBT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LAKEVIEW CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY
